title: Reis birthplace
wikitext:
[]: Rei's birthplace             Quote Post by LordCaldwell » Sat Jan 23, 2010 3:24 pm While this really isn't a debate worthy topic, I still find it interesting. In Rei's original birthplace, deep in Terminal Dogma, her room (if that's what you want to call it) seems to be covered in words like "strangeness" and molecular formulas (forgive me, Ive forgotten what they are) and other odd things. What could their function have been? Why such random words like strangeness? (although simoutaniously rather fitting, considering the nature of Rei's charachter) maybe there's just something I'm not realizing, as usuall. Even if there isn't a reason, which seems probable, I would still be interested in hearing what you guys have to say.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sat Jan 23, 2010 3:31 pm The stuff written on the walls is terminology related to quantum mechanics: http://wiki.evageeks.org/Rei%27s_Ghostly_Appearances_and_Quantum_Mechanics The wiki wrote: In Ep.23, when Ritsuko shows Shinji and Misato the room where Rei was born, the words "Top", "Bottom", and "Strangeness" are written on the wall. These are terms from Quantum Mechanics, and as it is emphasized that this was Rei's room, they must have been put there as a clue as to Rei's nature. Quantum Mechanics describes the behavior of subatomic particles. The basic subatomic particle, so far as we know, is the quark. Top and bottom are two of the six kinds of quarks known to exist. The reason that this is relevant is that quarks behave in ways that would be impossible in normal physics. For instance, a single quark can be in more than one place at a time. As we see in EoE, after absorbing Adam and re-joining with Lilith, Rei can be everywhere at once, collecting the souls of all mankind. Another property of quarks is that time has no meaning for them. At the sub atomic level, effects can happen before their causes, and particles can arrive at their destinations before starting on their journeys.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jan 23, 2010 4:47 pm Known quarks come in many difernet styles: up, down, top, bottom, strange and charm, to be exact. Why they felt the need to paint that on the wall is anybody's guess though.

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Sat Jan 23, 2010 9:47 pm How far has quantum physics even developed since 1995? I know a certain scientist received a Nobel for proving the existence of mesons in the 1940s, so I'm guessing it's been around a while but only started accelerating just recently. I can't recall another story that came before EVA that hints at quantum travel, or whatever it should be called. We have the transporters from Star Trek as an example, but I think that was more like dispersion and reassembly of atomic particles.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jan 23, 2010 10:01 pm Uriel Septim VII wrote: transporters from Star Trek

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Sat Jan 23, 2010 10:07 pm Hell, sometimes people even manage to have conscious sensory perceptions mid-transit, which is flat-out insane regardless of the methodology used.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jan 23, 2010 10:26 pm Uriel Septim VII wrote: Actually, Kirk would probably try to nail that space elf.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Tue Jan 26, 2010 4:55 pm Two possibilities: Majick rituals; or, What prisoners do.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Jan 26, 2010 11:35 pm I think all the writing on the walls is just there for dramatic effect. It’s just a way for them to get across the strangeness of Rei’s birthplace and also a way to drop hints as to her true nature.

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Wed Jan 27, 2010 12:31 am Maybe it's a metaphor for Rei's unquantifiable personality.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed Jan 27, 2010 12:48 am You make a good point. That could also be a reason for the writing on the walls.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaRaver » Wed Feb 10, 2010 5:48 am I always wondered/speculated if the writing on the walls of Rei's room were Fuyutsuki's notes from when he created her. Which would also provide some insight into her character and nature.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Wed Feb 10, 2010 10:01 am EvaRaver wrote: I always wondered/speculated if the writing on the walls of Rei's room were Fuyutsuki's notes from when he created her. Which would also provide some insight into her character and nature.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Wed Feb 10, 2010 10:23 am Allemann wrote: How is Fuyutsuki connected with Rei's origin? The man is nothing above a human key-chain.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Feb 10, 2010 10:26 am Allemann wrote: EvaRaver wrote: I always wondered/speculated if the writing on the walls of Rei's room were Fuyutsuki's notes from when he created her. Which would also provide some insight into her character and nature.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Feb 10, 2010 10:28 am Zuggy tossed that one out, didn't he? Fuyutsuki may have been speaking for the more recent iterations of Rei, rather than the original Rei I. Either that, or he figured out how to make the soup containing Yui's mortal remains and Lilith's soul coalesce into a physical body.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Feb 10, 2010 10:50 am How else would you possibly read that line? It seems pretty straightforward to me, and makes sense given his specialization in metaphysical biology. ...no comment on crazy symbolism stuff Zuggy dropped in there, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Wed Feb 10, 2010 10:55 am NemZ wrote: How else would you possibly read that line? It seems pretty straightforward to me, and makes sense given his specialization in metaphysical biology.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Feb 10, 2010 11:26 am I don't buy the 'accident' theory... not completely, anyway. Ritsuko tells us in 20 that Naoko was the one who came up with extraction process so surely she would have been present when it was attempted, but if Rei was created at this time it would be incompatible with the backstory in 21. Therefore some sort of further efforts must have been carried out in secret to produce Rei after the rescue attempt, using samples partially salvaged from the attempt. In all likelyhood they used a modified form of the Katsuragi experiment to make the first Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Feb 10, 2010 12:10 pm NemZ wrote: I don't buy the 'accident' theory... not completely, anyway. Ritsuko tells us in 20 that Naoko was the one who came up with extraction process so surely she would have been present when it was attempted, but if Rei was created at this time it would be incompatible with the backstory in 21. Therefore some sort of further efforts must have been carried out in secret to produce Rei after the rescue attempt, using samples partially salvaged from the attempt. In all likelyhood they used a modified form of the Katsuragi experiment to make the first Rei.

Article: Reis birthplace
 10, 2010 10:50 am How else would you possibly read that line? It seems pretty straightforward to me, and makes sense given his specialization in metaphysical biology. ...no comment on crazy symbolism stuff Zuggy dropped in there, though.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Feb 10, 2010 11:26 am I don't buy the 'accident' theory... not completely, anyway. Ritsuko tells us in 20 that Naoko was the one who came up with extraction process so surely she would have been present when it was attempted, but if Rei was created at this time it would be incompatible with the backstory in 21. Therefore some sort of further efforts must have been carried out in secret to produce Rei after the rescue attempt, using samples partially salvaged from the attempt. In all likelyhood they used a modified form of the Katsuragi experiment to make the first Rei.
